name: asgs
title: Azure Firewall — Application Security Groups
summary: Creates application security groups — workload-identity handles referenced by NSG rules. NIC membership and NSG rule wiring happen outside this module: associate NICs where the workloads are managed, and pass the ASG IDs output here into `azure/firewall/nsgs` rules.

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: resource_group_name
module.description: Name of the existing resource group for the ASGs.

module.name: location
module.description: Azure region for the ASGs. NIC associations require the NIC and ASG to be in the same region.

module.name: application_security_groups
module.description: Logical names of application security groups to create (e.g., [\"web\", \"app\", \"db\"]). NIC association happens where the NICs are managed — pass the resulting ASG IDs to NSG rules via the azure/firewall/nsgs module.

outputs.name: firewall_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical ASG name to application security group ID. Pass these into NSG rules (azure/firewall/nsgs) and NIC associations.

outputs.name: asg_names
outputs.description: Map of logical ASG name to ASG resource name.
